summary: Ads are losing money and measurement is broken. High CAC > AOV plus multiple attribution/count mismatches and request-to-checkout automation failures need urgent fixes before scaling spend.

findings.blocker: Ads unit economics are negative (CAC materially exceeds AOV).
findings.evidence: Google Ads 30d: spend=$1,668.80 clicks=511 ads_conversions=22 ads_cpa=$75.85 local_orders=18 local_net=$419.15 local_cac=$92.71; Supabase 30d totals: avgOrderValueAud=39.93 paid=27 netAud=998.25
findings.cac_impact: Current CAC ($75.85–$92.71) > Avg Order Value (~$39.93) => negative unit economics (loss per acquired customer).
findings.recommended_fix: Immediately stop/scale down paid acquisition driving these conversions until attribution is reconciled and offers/pricing or LTV assumptions change. Re-evaluate bids/campaign budgets; test only a tiny validation budget after fixes.

findings.blocker: Attribution/measurement mismatch and probable duplicate server-side conversions are inflating or misreporting conversions.
findings.evidence: PostHog 30d: google_ads_server_conversion count=100; Google Ads 30d: ads_conversions=22; Supabase 30d: adAttributed=26; PostHog 30d: purchase_completed_server=24 vs purchase_completed=6
findings.cac_impact: Misreported conversions can cause bidding engines to overbid and drive up CAC artificially; conversion counts disagree by factors (22 vs 26 vs 100) so cost-per-conversion and ROAS are unreliable.
findings.recommended_fix: Audit server-side conversion forwarding pipeline: deduplicate client/server events, add idempotency using a unique purchase ID (server and client), align event naming and windows with Google Ads, and stop forwarding synthetic/automated events. Reconcile one definitive purchase source (Supabase paid record) as ground truth.

findings.blocker: Large intake/request drop-offs; many partials are not converted and recovery is limited.
findings.evidence: Supabase 30d partial totals: count=119 converted=0 withEmail=21 recoverySent=6; Supabase 30d partial step certificate: count=75 converted=0; PostHog 30d: intake_started=384 checkout_viewed=75 purchase_completed_server=24
findings.cac_impact: High abandonment reduces conversion yield from ad traffic, increasing effective CAC (fewer paid customers per click).
findings.recommended_fix: Prioritise fixing request-to-checkout reliability and UX for the certificate flow, instrument immediate saves of email on partials, increase automated recovery sends, and capture gclid at first step to preserve attribution for recovery messages.

findings.blocker: Synthetic request-to-checkout automation failures point to environment/configuration issues (requests routed to localhost).
findings.evidence: Browser captures: medical-certificate request-to-checkout mobile: failed final=http://localhost:3060/request?service=med-cert consoleErrors=1; repeat-prescriptions request-to-checkout mobile: failed final=http://localhost:3060/request?service=repeat-script consoleErrors=1; erectile-dysfunction request-to-checkout mobile: failed final=http://localhost:3060/request?service=consult&subtype=ed consoleErrors=1; hair-loss request-to-checkout mobile: failed final=http://localhost:3060/request?service=consult&subtype=hair_loss consoleErrors=1
findings.cac_impact: If production submissions are failing similarly, conversion rates are suppressed and CAC increases; even if only synthetic, broken checks hide real regressions.
findings.recommended_fix: Fix the synthetic runner/config so tests hit production endpoints; re-run end-to-end checks. If production endpoints are implicated, remediate client-side submission endpoints so requests are sent to prod (not localhost) and remove any test-mode forwarding before re-enabling spend.

findings.blocker: Low recovery email coverage relative to partials; abandonedEmailSent appears small vs partial withEmail.
findings.evidence: Supabase 30d: abandonedEmailSent=8 partial totals: count=119 withEmail=21 recoverySent=6
findings.cac_impact: Low recovery throughput wastes existing acquisition spend — improving recovery could increase conversion rate and lower effective CAC.
findings.recommended_fix: Increase abandoned-cart/recovery email triggers and cadence, ensure gclid/email captured at first step, and A/B test recovery timing and subject lines. Use the 21 withEmail partials as primary recovery targets.

findings.blocker: Revenue and conversion counts do not reconcile across systems (Google Ads, PostHog, Supabase).
findings.evidence: Google Ads 30d local_net=$419.15 local_orders=18; Supabase 30d netAud=998.25 paid=27; PostHog 30d purchase_completed_server=24 purchase_completed=6
findings.cac_impact: Inaccurate revenue attribution prevents accurate CAC/LTV calculation and misguides budget decisions.
findings.recommended_fix: Establish single source of truth for revenue (Supabase paid rows), backfill gclid or conversion IDs into that source, and reconcile daily with Ads and PostHog. Add alerts for mismatched conversion counts > X%.

ads_triage: Pause or heavily cap Google search/conversion campaigns until attribution and server-side conversion deduplication are fixed (evidence: Google Ads 30d ads_cpa=$75.85 local_cac=$92.71 vs AOV ~$39.93).
Do not create new bid or conversion-based optimisations until conversion events are reconciled (PostHog google_ads_server_conversion=100 vs Google ads_conversions=22).
If immediate pause is not acceptable, reduce daily budgets materially and run a small validation budget only after fixes.
measurement_notes: Server vs client duplication: PostHog shows google_ads_server_conversion=100 (30d) while Google reports 22 conversions and Supabase adAttributed=26 — investigate server-side forwarding deduplication and naming.
Purchase counts mismatch: Supabase paid=27 (30d) vs PostHog purchase_completed_server=24 vs purchase_completed=6 — determine canonical purchase event and enforce idempotent reporting.
Synthetic monitoring misrouting: multiple request-to-checkout captures ended at http://localhost:3060 — fix runner/environment to ensure synthetic tests exercise production paths.

Partial-intake capture: many partials (partial totals count=119) but low recoverySent (6) and withEmail=21 — tracking exists for partials but recovery pipeline is underutilised; ensure gclid/email captured at first step for recovery attribution.
